Publications

Published

April 1, 2026

Peer-reviewed articles

D. Lusk, S. Wolf, et al. (2026). Crowdsourced biodiversity monitoring fills gaps in global plant trait mapping. Nature Communications, 17, (1).
H. A. Chandanpurkar, J. S. Famiglietti, et al. (2025). Unprecedented continental drying, shrinking freshwater availability, and increasing land contributions to sea level rise. Science Advances, 11, (30).
M. Zeng, E. A. Baptista, and K. Kakinuma. (2024). Heat impacts on an aging society: A spatio-temporal analysis of heatstroke deaths in japan. Environmental Research Communications, 6, (11), 115003.
K. Kakinuma and Y. Wada. (2024). Gender inequality in global water security. Environmental Research Letters, 19, (11), 114071.
N. Takashina and K. Kakinuma. (2024). Modeling a pastoral social–ecological system and its application to climate risk assessment of traditional livelihoods. Sustainability Science, 19, (3), 1079–1096.
K. Kakinuma, K. Tamura, et al. (2023). Economic inequality expanded after an extreme climate event: A long-term analysis of herders’ household data in mongolia. Sustainability Science, 19, (1), 275–283.
K. Kakinuma and G. J. Abel. (2022). The development of internal migration flow patterns in japan. Regional Studies, Regional Science, 9, (1), 669–671.
M. Elsamadony, M. Fujii, et al. (2022). Preliminary quantitative assessment of the multidimensional impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sustainable development goals. Journal of Cleaner Production, 372, 133812.
I. Biurrun, R. Pielech, et al. (2021). Benchmarking plant diversity of palaearctic grasslands and other open habitats. Journal of Vegetation Science, 32, (4).
K. Kakinuma, M. J. Puma, et al. (2020). Flood-induced population displacements in the world. Environmental Research Letters, 15, (12), 124029.
E. A. Baptista, K. Kakinuma, and B. L. Queiroz. (2020). Association between cardiovascular mortality and economic development: A spatio-temporal study for prefectures in japan. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 17, (4), 1311.
K. M. Lukman, J. M. D. Quevedo, et al. (2019). Indonesia provincial spatial plans on mangroves in era of decentralization: Application of content analysis to 27 provinces and “blue carbon” as overlooked components. Journal of Forest Research, 24, (6), 341–348.
K. Kakinuma, A. Yanagawa, et al. (2019). Socio-ecological interactions in a changing climate: A review of the mongolian pastoral system. Sustainability, 11, (21), 5883.
M. J. Puma, S. Y. Chon, et al. (2018). A developing food crisis and potential refugee movements. Nature Sustainability, 1, (8), 380–382.
T. Endo, K. Kakinuma, et al. (2018). Are water markets globally applicable? Environmental Research Letters.
J. Dengler, V. Wagner, et al. (2018). GrassPlot - a database of multi-scale plant diversity in palaearctic grasslands. Phytocoenologia, 48, (3), 331–347.
K. Kakinuma, A. Terui, et al. (2017). Detection of vegetation trends in highly variable environments after grazing exclusion in mongolia. Journal of Vegetation Science, 28, (5), 965–974.
N. Hanasaki, S. Yoshikawa, et al. (2016). A seawater desalination scheme for global hydrological models. Hydrology and Earth System Sciences Discussions, 1–25.
N. Hanasaki, S. Yoshikawa, et al. (2016). A seawater desalination scheme for global hydrological models. Hydrology and Earth System Sciences, 20, (10), 4143–4157.
S. AMNATSAN, Y. ISERI, et al. (2016). MONTHLY RESERVOIR INFLOW FORECASTING IN THAILAND: A COMPARISON OF ANN-BASED AND HISTORICAL ANALOUGE-BASED METHODS. Journal of Japan Society of Civil Engineers, Ser. B1 (Hydraulic Engineering), 72, (4), I_7–I_12.
J. H. Lee, K. Kakinuma, et al. (2015). Coupled social and ecological dynamics of herders in mongolian rangelands. Ecological Economics, 114, 208–217.
K. Kakinuma, T. Okayasu, et al. (2014). Herding strategies during a drought vary at multiple scales in mongolian rangeland. Journal of Arid Environments, 109, 88–91.
K. Kakinuma, T. Sasaki, et al. (2014). Relationship between pastoralists’ evaluation of rangeland state and vegetation threshold changes in mongolian rangelands. Environmental Management, 54, (4), 888–896.
T. Sasaki, K. Kakinuma, and Y. Yoshihara. (2013). Marmot disturbance drives trait variations among five dominant grasses in a mongolian grassland. Rangeland Ecology & Management, 66, (4), 487–491.
K. Kakinuma, T. Okayasu, et al. (2013). Rangeland management in highly variable environments: Resource variations across the landscape mediate the impact of grazing on vegetation in mongolia. Grassland Science, 59, (1), 44–51.
T. Sasaki, T. Ohkuro, et al. (2013). Vegetation in a post-ecological threshold state may not recover after short-term livestock exclusion in mongolian rangelands. Arid Land Research and Management, 27, (1), 101–110.
K. Kakinuma and S. Takatsuki. (2012). Applying local knowledge to rangeland management in northern mongolia: Do “narrow plants” reflect the carrying capacity of the land? Pastoralism: Research, Policy and Practice, 2, (1), 23.
K. Kakinuma, T. Ozaki, et al. (2008). How pastoralists in mongolia perceive vegetation changes caused by grazing. Nomadic Peoples, 12, (2), 67–73.
